text: Grind all valves to remove the pits and carbon. Valve face angle: 45.5°
Resurface valve seats with 45° carbide cutter. Remove only enough metal to clean seat.
Correct the seat position. To correct seating that is too high, use 30° and 45° cutters. If seating is too low, use 65° and 45° cutters.
Check valve concentricity. Lightly coat seat with prussian blue. Install valve and rotate. If blue appears 360° around face, valve stem and face are concentric. If not, replace valve.
Check seat/guide concentricity. Apply a light coat of prussian blue on valve face. Install and rotate valve. If blue appears 360° around valve seat, guide and seat are concentric. If not, recut seat.
Turn crankshaft 360° and align timing mark.

text: Heat piston in piston heater to about 100°C (212°F) and remove piston pin (Fig. 4-124).
Heat piston to 100°C (212°F), coat pin with engine oil. The pin should be able to be pushed into piston hole with thumb pressure (Fig. 4-126).
Measure piston diameter at right angle to piston pin center line. Measurement must be made at normal temperature (20°C or 68°F) (Fig. 4-134).
Heat piston to about 100° (212°F) (Fig. 4-142).
